title: Discrete-time hazard model with survival-consistent features
summary: Pit-on-next-lap is naturally a one-step hazard: probability of stopping at lap t+1 conditional on having not stopped yet in the current stint.
rationale: Pit-on-next-lap is naturally a one-step hazard: probability of stopping at lap t+1 conditional on having not stopped yet in the current stint. Survival literature suggests discrete-time hazard formulations are better aligned than plain independent row classification for event timing problems.
implementation_hint: Create stint-relative hazard features such as lap-in-stint, current stint survival age, time-since-last-stop, and piecewise lap bins; train a compact hazard specialist (logistic/CatBoost) only on rows before the terminal pit of each stint, then blend its rank with the current best classifier. Validate with rolling or grouped folds by race-driver so future laps never inform earlier hazards.
expected_effect: Better ranking near true stop windows and fewer false spikes on laps that are impossible or too early for a stop.
risk: This overlaps prior event-time ideas, so it must be a stricter survival-consistent implementation rather than just adding more lag features.
